records.ety: [F. mystifier, fr. Gr. <?/ + L. -ficare (in comp.) to make. See 1st Mystery, and -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mystified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Mystifying (?).]

defs: To involve in mystery; to make obscure or difficult to understand; as, to mystify a passage of Scripture.
To perplex the mind of; to puzzle; to impose upon the credulity of; to baffle; as, to mystify an opponent.
quotes: He took undue advantage of his credulity and mystified him exceedingly.
